name: Zerodha Trade Execution — CQRS
scenario: Zerodha's portfolio dashboard loads in 800ms because it joins 5 tables on every request. Refactor using CQRS: separate write (trade execution) and read (portfolio dashboard) models.

core.description: PlaceOrderCommand record + CancelOrderCommand record. Both immutable, validate-by-construction (null checks in compact constructor).
OrderCommandHandler @Service: validate command, save to write DB, publish event via ApplicationEventPublisher. Returns orderId String.
PortfolioProjection @Service: @EventListener on OrderFilledEvent, updates in-memory Map<merchantId, totalValue> (or repository). @Async optional.
GetPortfolioQuery record + PortfolioQueryHandler @Service: reads from projection's store (no DB joins). Returns PortfolioView record.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): valid command→orderId, invalid command throws, projection updates→query returns correct value. All 3 pass.
README: explains eventual consistency — when dashboard shows old value and why this is acceptable.

bonus.description: Multiple projections: AnalyticsProjection counts daily trade volume per symbol. Same OrderFilledEvent updates both PortfolioProjection and AnalyticsProjection independently.
Command validation: CommandValidationException for invalid inputs (separate from IllegalArgumentException). @ControllerAdvice returns 422 Unprocessable Entity.
Paginated trade history: GetTradeHistoryQuery(merchantId, page, size) + handler returning Page<TradeHistoryView>.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Command returns only ID (not the entity): commands are write-only — the response is just 'accepted' with the orderId. Use GET /portfolio/{id} to read state after a command. ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent(): Spring's built-in event bus — works within the same JVM/service. Projection @EventListener: fires synchronously by default (easier for tests). Add @Async + @EnableAsync only if you want non-blocking behavior. Eventual consistency: the test for 'projection updates → query returns correct value' should call the command then the query in the same test (synchronous event). PortfolioView record: immutable data carrier for the read side — NOT a JPA entity. Read model entities are separate from write model entities.
